ideation: This sequence simulates the action of gently placing a crown on one's head. It begins with the arms at rest and concludes with both hands positioned as if holding a crown above the head. The movement is slow and deliberate, emphasizing the regal nature of the gesture. The left arm will primarily handle the crown, while the right arm provides support and balance. In the first frame, both arms hang naturally at the sides. In frame two, the left arm begins to rise, rotating outward and upward to reach toward the head. In frame three, the right arm moves to support the left, creating a stable base for the crown-placing action. In frame four, both arms gently hold the crown above the head. In the final frame, the arms maintain their position, holding the crown steadily overhead. This sequence is designed to convey a sense of majesty and careful placement, reflecting the significance of the crown.

frames.beat: arms hang at rest, ready for royalty
left arm lifts, reaching for the crown's placement
right arm rises to support the royal gesture
both hands gently cradle the crown overhead
holding the crown steady, a regal pose
